codeReview.summary: The new hardening suite is substantial, but two required behaviors are still not actually proven: the parent-abort path is still mocked instead of driven through a real bridge teardown path, and the pipeline/conjunction rejection case cannot detect partial pre-rejection execution.

issues.description: The parent-abort scenario never exercises a real parent teardown. The test replaces the bridge context with a `createContext` double and manually rejects a deferred from inside `prompt()` (`packages/webapp/tests/scoops/agent-hardening.test.ts:322-370`), so it only proves that `AgentBridge.spawn()` cleans up after an already-rejected promise. In production, the bridge-owned context is kept only in a local variable (`packages/webapp/src/scoops/agent-bridge.ts:313-329`) and bridge scoops are registered only through `registerExistingScoop()` (`packages/webapp/src/scoops/orchestrator.ts:295-305`), while `Orchestrator.shutdown()` only tears down entries from `this.contexts` (`packages/webapp/src/scoops/orchestrator.ts:68,1272-1273`). A real `orch.shutdown()` / parent-process teardown still has no path to stop an in-flight bridge scoop, so the required 'abort mid-run -> no hang + cleanup' behavior is still unproven and likely still broken.
issues.suggestedFix: Expose a real abort path for bridge-spawned contexts (for example by registering them in orchestrator teardown or threading an abort signal into `AgentBridge.spawn()`), then replace the mocked rejection with a test that drives that real abort/shutdown path against a real `ScoopContext`.

issues.description: The pipeline/conjunction rejection test does not actually prove whole-line atomicity. Its rejected command is `cat /shared/x | wc -l && rm -rf /shared/` (`packages/webapp/tests/scoops/agent-hardening.test.ts:611-665`), so every segment before the disallowed `rm` is read-only. The assertions only check that `/shared/x` stayed unchanged and that the error text mentions `rm`; they never assert that earlier segments produced no output or that an earlier write-capable segment was prevented. A regression that still runs `cat | wc` before rejecting `rm` would still pass, so the feature's 'reject the whole line / no partial file writes' requirement is not fully covered.
issues.suggestedFix: Use a rejected line with an earlier allow-listed write (or another observable side effect) and assert that neither that side effect nor any successful output appears when a later segment is disallowed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `webapp-worker` says a feature's `verificationSteps` define done, but also says manual CDP verification is optional for pure vitest-only features and should be documented in `whatWasLeftUndone` when skipped. This feature still listed a manual smoke step in `features.json`, yet the handoff reported `interactiveChecks: []`, `whatWasLeftUndone: ""`, and `followedProcedure: true`.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: Clarifying which rule wins—and how workers should record a skipped feature-listed manual step—would make future handoffs auditable without scrutiny having to infer whether the omission was acceptable. Evidence: `features.json:1422-1424`, handoff `2026-04-18T00-03-23-950Z__error-and-abort-hardening__df88d78b-df40-46cc-90ee-c0e425b723cd.json:12,46,97`, `.factory/skills/webapp-worker/SKILL.md:31,90,143`.

summary: Review failed. The new hardening suite adds broad coverage, but it still does not exercise a real parent-abort path and its atomic-rejection case cannot catch partial pre-rejection execution, so two mission-critical behaviors remain unproven.
